programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Innovative Eurocup Draw, Electric Vehicles & Digital Security"
programme.sub-title: Innovative Eurocup Draw, Electric Vehicles & Digital Security
programme.desc: Catch Hamburg’s innovative Euro 2024 draw, a device boosting transporters in winter, and insights from a digital security fair.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Mikey McBryan"
programme.desc: Anthony chats with the star of TV’s ‘Ice Pilots’.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Day in the Life of an Airline Pilot"
programme.sub-title: Day in the Life of an Airline Pilot
programme.desc: Raeleen shows us what a typical day is like for her as an airline first officer.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Cross Border Flying"
programme.desc: Anthony flies the 206 from Canada to the United States and checks in with Customs.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S1 - "Terry's Criminal Mind"
programme.desc: A murderer tries to kill his final victim as the car they are in slowly sinks to the bottom of a lake. The only way to save the victim is to rescue her as the car continues to sink.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S1 - "Shark Go Boom!"
programme.desc: Here, Chief Brody hangs on to his sinking boat as a giant shark approaches him with a scuba tank lodged in its mouth. We construct a shark on a platform to test if you can hit the tank with an M1 Garand rifle while perched in a precarious position.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S1 - "The Good the Bad and Terry"
programme.sub-title: The Good the Bad and Terry
programme.desc: In this classic western, Clint Eastwood – aka “Blondie” – saves Eli Wallach from the hangman’s noose three separate times by shooting and severing the rope that he hangs from. We test the reality of these shots with the weapons that were used.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "CUBICLE"
programme.desc: A futuristic designer, Robert Propst, dreamed up “The Action Office” as a workplace utopia that would unleash the modern knowledge worker’s vast untapped potential.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "BARCODE"
programme.desc: Barcodes have radically changed global commerce, helping fuel the rise of everything from massive companies like Walmart to world powers like China. And it all started with a man daydreaming on a beach in 1949.

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Fighting Mortality"
programme.desc: What is the secret to eternal life? Alie Ward goes from an ancient herbal remedy to arrive at embryonic stem cells and the tantalising prospect of a future where we could be forever young.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Raining Fish"
programme.desc: Alex is in Australia’s Northern Territory where a bizarre weather phenomenon took place. Hundreds of fish were reported to have rained down on the outback.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Spook Lights"
programme.desc: Alex Hannaford heads to America to investigate reports of balls of light, known as Spook Lights, floating in the night sky above a remote country road.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Tasmanian Tiger"
programme.desc: Alex seeks the truth behind a reported sightings of the continent’s biggest marsupial carnivore; an elusive wolf-like creature called the Tasmanian Tiger.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Toads"
programme.desc: Investigative journalist Alex Hannaford heads to Hamburg to uncover the truth behind bizarre headlines of exploding toads.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "New York"
programme.desc: New York, New York. A city so nice, they named it twice. The cultural and financial capital of the world. Join us as we navigate the gridlocked streets and canyons created by Manhattan’s skyscrapers and marvel at the engineering wonder of the subway.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Paris"
programme.desc: The celebration of culture and conservation of heritage underpin this episode on the City of Light. Home to engineering marvels, such as the Eiffel Tower and the glass pyramid entrance to the Louvre, Paris holds a special place in our imaginations.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Tokyo"
programme.desc: Home to more than 37 million people, Greater Tokyo is the largest city in the world. To say it’s crowded is nothing short of an understatement. Join us as we navigate this gargantuan yet liveable city that still manages to function on a human scale.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Sydney"
programme.desc: Sydney is a city defined by its harbour: the sapphire jewel in Australia’s crown. Established as a penal colony in 1788, it remains a city of immigrants’ intent on “bending the rules”. It’s brash, it’s crass and it’s full of engineering marvels.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S1 - "Memory"
programme.desc: Jack meets the world memory champion and a man who can remember every day of his life.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S1 - "Faces"
programme.desc: Jack meets a man who is unable to recognise faces and puts a super-recogniser to the test.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S1 - "Phantom Limb"
programme.desc: Jack joins amputees trying to find out why they feel sensations in their missing limbs.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S1 - "Seeing is Believing"
programme.desc: Jack meets a man who has bizarre hallucinations and witnesses cutting edge surgery.

programme.title: Extraordinary Humans S1 - "Super-Brain / Music Master"
programme.desc: Thom meets Gabriel Dechichi who solves Rubik’s cubes whilst blindfolded, and Derek Paravacini who can memorise an entire piece of music after a single hearing.

programme.title: The World of Bridges S1 - "China"
programme.desc: We visit China's most famous Wind and Rain Bridge and the Ponte dei Salti in the Verzasca Valley that literally dates back to the Roman era.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Hurricane Triple Threat"
programme.desc: Early September of 2008 saw radical imagery on the hurricane maps of the Atlantic – four named tropical storms, swirling in a train across the ocean. “Angry Planet” was there as three of them made landfall as hurricanes in the US.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Elephant Cave"
programme.desc: Possibly the home of the deadly Marberg Ebola virus, Kitum Cave is high on Mt.Elgon straddling the Kenya-Uganda border. When host George Kourounis gets bitten by one of the bats…well, you’ll have to see the show to find out what happens.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Eco-Disasters"
programme.desc: No-one creates eco-disasters on quite the scale of the former Soviet Union. In Uzbekistan, the Aral Sea, once one of the largest freshwater lakes in the world, has seen its waters diverted, so that it has shrunk to 1/6 its previous size.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Costa Rica"
programme.desc: George explores the volcanoes of Costa Rica with an international team of scientists, rafts down whitewater rivers in full flood, canyoneers down raging waterfalls, and handles some of deadliest snakes this tropical country has to offer.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Antarctica"
programme.desc: George returns to “Fin Del Mundo” at the southern tip of Argentina. After braving the stormy Drake passage, he explores volcanic Deception Island, camps out in the Antarctic snow, and paddles a kayak through icebergs calved off the melting glaciers.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Arctic Winter"
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers in the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. First uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Polynesian Volcanoes"
programme.desc: Filming the volcanoes, mudpools and geysers in the geothermic wonderland of New Zealand’s north island, we learn that a new island is being created by an undersea volcano 1000 miles north in Tonga

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Highway to Hell"
programme.desc: George heads straight into the “Bearcage”, looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones that Texas and Oklahoma can throw at them. He finds it –and the stormchasing vehicles don’t look too pretty after coming through these massive storms

programme.title: Angry Planet S3 - "Space"
programme.desc: We break the bounds of earth, and look at the environment of space. We examine the difficulties and challenges of exploring this most challenging environment.
